description_long: This is a weathered stone grave marker set on a rectangular base, surrounded by patches of grass and earth. The front face features an engraved inscription commemorating Minnie Jones, the wife of James Jones, with birth and death dates clearly visible. The sides of the monument are adorned with carved relief crosses, adding a solemn decorative element. The back of the marker is plain and unadorned, showing signs of age and exposure to the elements. The stone surface is rough and matte, with visible chipping and discoloration from years outdoors. The overall structure is solid and heavy, typical of traditional cemetery headstones.
description: A weathered stone grave marker with engraved inscription and carved crosses on the sides, set on a rectangular base with patches of grass.
description_view_0: The front view shows the engraved inscription for Minnie Jones, with clear text and some grass at the base.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a carved cross in relief and the thickness of the stone, with visible grass around the base.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, unadorned stone surface with signs of weathering and no inscriptions.
description_view_3: The right side view features another carved cross and the monument's rectangular profile, with more grass at the base.
attribute_tombstone_inscriptions: OF MINNIE JONES THE WIFE OF JAMES JONES BORN IN COLUMBUS GA DEC 25 1857 DIED NOV 21 1907
